subject: UX & Research
title: Cognitive Load and Simplicity
description: A quick, practical guide to Cognitive Load and Simplicity.

hook.text: A form splits your phone number into three boxes: area code, prefix, line. One field would be simpler.

buildup.text: Cognitive load is the mental effort to use an interface. More steps and choices mean more load.

discovery.text: 800 million people have cognitive disabilities: dyslexia, ADHD, autism, and brain injuries among them.

twist.text: Cognitive challenges are also situational. Sleep loss, stress, and multitasking reduce everyone's capacity.

climax.text: Reduce steps, simplify language, and show one thing at a time. What helps cognitive disabilities helps all.

punchline.text: Simpler is more accessible. Reduce steps and choices for everyone.

quiz.question: Why does reducing cognitive load benefit all users, not just those with disabilities?
options: Situational factors like stress, fatigue, and multitasking reduce everyone's cognitive capacity
All users have the same cognitive abilities
Cognitive load only affects mobile users
